title: JS: Filter Array of Objects
content:
Let's say we have this ... 
const runCmd = [
  { scope: 'js', cmd: 'babel-node' },
  { scope: 'ruby', cmd: 'ruby -W0' },
  { scope: 'php', cmd: 'php -f' },
  { scope: 'python', cmd: 'python' },
  { scope: 'shell', cmd: 'bash' }
]
And we want to get the `cmd` for each language scope, except for Javascript. (Say, we have another check for `js` but not `jsx`.) We could either do this. 
runCmd
  .filter(function(rc) {
    return rc.scope !== 'js'
  })
  .forEach(function(rc) {
    console.log(rc.cmd)
  })
Or we can do this. 
runCmd.filter(rc => rc.scope !== 'js').forEach(rc => console.log(rc.cmd))
Actual use of the script ... https://gist.github.com/macsf/f27537cd312c2796522884025106e33a Hmmm.. (This is the reason why I like Atom more than VSCode. It is so easy whipping up a quick script for some simple thing. I don't need to write a whole plugin.)
